name: Slite Team Knowledge Base API
what_it_does: Slite team knowledge base REST API for remote and distributed teams to create, organize, and search documentation with AI-powered Ask feature for instant answer retrieval from team knowledge. Enables AI agents to manage document creation and channel organization for team wiki automation, handle AI-powered document search and question answering for knowledge retrieval automation, access document version history and collaborative editing for documentation lifecycle automation, retrieve document analytics and view tracking for content effectiveness automation, manage team member access and workspace permissions for knowledge governance automation, handle document template library for standardized documentation automation, access integration with Slack, Notion, Confluence, and GitHub for knowledge migration automation, retrieve linked document and reference tracking for knowledge graph automation, manage table and database content within documents for structured data documentation automation, and integrate Slite with Slack, Zapier, and collaboration platforms for end-to-end distributed team knowledge management.
use_cases: Creating team wikis from distributed team documentation agents
Answering team questions from AI knowledge retrieval agents
Migrating docs from platform consolidation agents
Tracking document usage from content analytics agents
not_for: Enterprise knowledge management with expert verification (use Guru for verified knowledge)
Customer-facing documentation and help center (use Intercom or Zendesk Guide)
Code documentation and developer-specific wikis (use GitBook or Docusaurus)
best_when: A remote or distributed team wanting AI agents to automate team wiki creation, AI-powered knowledge retrieval, document organization, and cross-platform knowledge consolidation through Slite's remote-first knowledge platform.
avoid_when: AI ASK FEATURE QUALITY DEPENDS ON DOCUMENTATION DENSITY: Slite's AI Ask feature answers questions from existing documentation; automated knowledge retrieval quality depends on documentation coverage and freshness; automated Q&A via Slite AI for topics not well-documented in workspace returns low-confidence or no answers; documentation quality investment required before AI retrieval automation delivers value. CHANNEL ORGANIZATION FLAT vs NESTED STRUCTURE: Slite uses Channels and Notes organizational model (not deeply nested hierarchy); automated documentation organization for complex hierarchical content must adapt to Slite's flatter organizational model; automated migration of deeply nested wikis from Confluence creates structural mismatch requiring reorganization. API SCOPE PRIMARILY READ — LIMITED WRITE AUTOMATION: Slite's public API is more comprehensive for read operations than write; automated content creation via API may have more limitations than read/search; automated documentation generation workflows must verify write API coverage for required document operations before committing to Slite as automation target.

auth.notes: Slite uses API key and OAuth 2.0 for integrations. REST API with JSON. Paris, France HQ. Founded 2017 by Christophe Pasquier and Ibrahim Böhme. Backed by Notion investor Lachy Groom, Point Nine, Kima Ventures ($12M+ raised). Products: Team knowledge base, AI Ask, Channels/Notes organization, document templates, integrations. Platforms: Slack, Notion, Confluence, GitHub, Google Docs. GDPR. Remote-first team focus. Competes with Notion, Confluence, and Slab for team knowledge management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free Standard plan (unlimited docs, limited AI Ask credits, basic features)
pricing.notes: Paris France. Point Nine/Kima backed. Free tier + paid plans. GDPR. Remote-team focus.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; search and document queries throttled; no webhooks — polling for change detection

known_agent_gotchas: NO WEBHOOKS — POLLING REQUIRED FOR CHANGE DETECTION: Slite does not provide webhooks for document update notifications; automated workflows that need to detect documentation changes must implement polling; high-frequency polling for document change detection wastes API rate limit budget; implement reasonable polling interval (hourly or daily) for documentation sync automation

AI ASK CREDITS CONSUMPTION IN AUTOMATED QUERIES: Slite AI Ask feature consumes credits on paid plans; automated AI knowledge retrieval workflows using Slite Ask must monitor credit consumption; automated high-frequency AI Ask queries create unexpected credit depletion; implement AI Ask query budgeting in automated knowledge retrieval workflows

DOCUMENT PERMISSION INHERITANCE FOR AUTOMATED ACCESS: Slite document permissions inherit from Channel permissions by default; automated document access must verify Channel permission grants correct access level; automated document read workflows that bypass Channel permission check encounter permission denied for documents in restricted Channels

RICH TEXT FORMAT FOR AUTOMATED CONTENT CREATION: Slite documents use rich text format (not plain text); automated document generation must format content in Slite's rich text schema; automated documentation generation using plain text creates improperly formatted notes that lose structure and readability in Slite viewer

NOTE vs DOCUMENT vs TEMPLATE ENDPOINT DISTINCTION: Slite API distinguishes between Notes (user-created content), Templates (reusable structure), and Channels (organization containers); automated content creation must use correct endpoint type; automated template instantiation requires separate API call from note creation; automated migration treating all content as Notes misses template and Channel structure
agent_readiness.security_notes: Team knowledge base. GDPR. API key/OAuth2. EU. Team documentation and knowledge data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Knowledge base. GDPR. API key/OAuth2. EU. Team documentation data.
